title: Implement temporal.testing.history namespace
content:
# Implement temporal.testing.history namespace

Implements [[s-5c1w]].

## Overview

Create `src/temporal/testing/history.clj` â€” the foundational history-loading and capture namespace. This has no dependencies on other new issues.

## Functions to implement

| Function | Wraps | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|
| `from-json` | `WorkflowExecutionHistory/fromJson(String)` | 2-arity `[json workflow-id]` overload for explicit id override |
| `from-file` | slurp â†’ `fromJson` | Accept `java.io.File`, `java.nio.Path`, or string path |
| `from-resource` | `(io/resource name)` â†’ slurp â†’ `fromJson` | Throw clear `ex-info` if resource not found |
| `fetch` | `(.fetchHistory ^WorkflowClient client workflow-id)` | `client` is a temporal.client.core WorkflowClient |
| `->json` | `(.toJson history pretty-print?)` | Default `pretty-print?` = `true` |

## Datafiable extension

```clojure
(extend-protocol p/Datafiable
  WorkflowExecutionHistory
  (datafy [h]
    {:workflow-id (.. h getWorkflowExecution getWorkflowId)
     :run-id      (.. h getWorkflowExecution getRunId)
     :events      (.getEvents h)}))
```

## Java imports needed

```clojure
(import '[io.temporal.internal.common WorkflowExecutionHistory])
```

No new Maven dependency â€” `WorkflowExecutionHistory` is bundled in `temporal-shaded 1.32.1`.

## Conventions

- Namespace docstring: `"Methods for loading, capturing, and serializing Temporal workflow execution histories for use in replay testing."`
- All public fns: markdown docstring with one-line summary, `Arguments:` table, fenced `clojure` example, `[[cross-refs]]` to related fns
- Internal helpers: `^:no-doc`
- Model on `src/temporal/testing/env.clj`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 5 functions implemented and documented
- [ ] `Datafiable` extension: `(d/datafy (history/from-json json-str))` returns map
- [ ] `from-resource` throws clear `ex-info` when resource not found
- [ ] `from-file` accepts string, `File`, and `Path`
- [ ] No new Maven dependencies

title: Implement temporal.testing.replayer core (create/replay/close/replay-history)
content:
# Implement temporal.testing.replayer core

Implements [[s-5c1w]]. Blocked by [[i-850r]].

## Overview

Create `src/temporal/testing/replayer.clj`. Provides a reusable replayer handle (TestWorkflowEnvironment + worker via dynamic-dispatch path) and single-history replay with Clojure-friendly error handling.

## `replayer-options` spec-map (internal)

Follow the SDK idiom from `src/temporal/internal/utils.clj`:

```clojure
(def ^:no-doc replayer-option-spec {})  ;; extend if WorkflowReplayerOptions has useful builder methods

(defn ^:no-doc replayer-options-> [params]
  (u/build (WorkflowReplayerOptions/newBuilder) replayer-option-spec params))
```

Expose `replayer-options` as a public var (the spec map) for documentation, matching `temporal.client.worker/worker-options`.

## `create` â€” reusable replayer handle

Options map:
| Key | Description | Type | Default |
|-----|-------------|------|---------|
| `:dispatch` | Explicit workflow dispatch table | vector of symbols | auto-dispatch |
| `:data-converter` | Custom data converter | DataConverter | default |
| `:replayer-options` | WorkflowReplayerOptions params | map | `{}` |

- Build a `TestWorkflowEnvironment` (thread `:data-converter` through `workflow-client-options` as `temporal.testing.env/create` does)
- Get worker: `(.newWorker env task-queue worker-opts)`
- Call `temporal.client.worker/init` on the worker with dispatch table
- Start: `(.start env)`
- Return a `Closeable` record holding `{:env env :worker worker}`
- Use a generated internal task queue name (e.g. `(str "replay-" (java.util.UUID/randomUUID))`)

## `replay` â€” single history

```clojure
(defn replay [replayer history]
  (try
    (WorkflowReplayer/replayWorkflowExecution history (:worker replayer))
    (catch Exception e
      (throw (ex-info "Workflow replay failed: non-determinism detected"
                      {:workflow-id (.. history getWorkflowExecution getWorkflowId)
                       :cause e})))))
```

## `close` / `synchronized-stop`

- `close` â†’ async: `(.shutdown (:env replayer))`
- `synchronized-stop` â†’ blocking: `(.close (:env replayer))`
- `Closeable.close()` delegates to `synchronized-stop`

## `replay-history` â€” one-shot convenience

```clojure
(defn replay-history
  ([history] (replay-history history {}))
  ([history opts]
   (with-open [r (create opts)]
     (replay r history))))
```

## Java imports

```clojure
(import '[io.temporal.testing WorkflowReplayer TestWorkflowEnvironment])
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `create` builds a working env with dynamic dispatch registered
- [ ] `replay` passes on valid history, throws `ex-info` on non-determinism
- [ ] `with-open` works (Closeable implemented)
- [ ] `replay-history` one-shot works
- [ ] `:data-converter` and `:dispatch` options work correctly
- [ ] No new Maven dependencies

title: Implement replay-all and datafied ReplayResults
content:
# Implement replay-all and datafied ReplayResults

Implements [[s-5c1w]]. Blocked by [[i-b5o1]].

## Overview

Add `replay-all` to `temporal.testing.replayer` for batch replay (the CI use case), and add `Datafiable` protocol extensions for `ReplayResults` and `ReplayResults$ReplayError`.

## `replay-all` function

```clojure
(defn replay-all
  ([replayer histories] (replay-all replayer histories {}))
  ([replayer histories {:keys [fail-fast?] :or {fail-fast? false}}]
   (-> (WorkflowReplayer/replayWorkflowExecutions histories fail-fast? (:worker replayer))
       d/datafy)))
```

Options:
| Key | Description | Type | Default |
|-----|-------------|------|---------|
| `:fail-fast?` | Stop on first failure | boolean | `false` |

Returns datafied map: `{:total n :failures [{:workflow-id "..." :error #<Exception>}]}`

## Datafiable extensions

```clojure
(extend-protocol p/Datafiable
  ReplayResults
  (datafy [r]
    {:total    (+ (count (.getReplayErrors r)) (count (.getSuccessfulExecutions r)))
     :failures (mapv d/datafy (.getReplayErrors r))})

  ReplayResults$ReplayError
  (datafy [e]
    {:workflow-id (.getWorkflowId e)
     :error       (.getException e)}))
```

Verify actual method names on `ReplayResults` via `javap` before implementing.

## Java imports

```clojure
(import '[io.temporal.testing WorkflowReplayer ReplayResults])
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Successful batch returns `{:total n :failures []}`
- [ ] Failed batch includes offending workflow-id in `:failures`
- [ ] `fail-fast? true` stops on first failure
- [ ] `fail-fast? false` collects all failures
- [ ] `(datafy results)` works at the REPL
- [ ] Return value is a plain Clojure map

title: Add tests for replay testing API
content:
# Add tests for replay testing API

Implements [[s-5c1w]]. Blocked by [[i-b5o1]] and [[i-9rsa]].

## New test files

### `test/temporal/test/replay_history_test.clj`
Unit tests for `temporal.testing.history` (no live Temporal environment needed):

- `from-json` â†’ `->json` round-trip: parse JSON string, serialize back, re-parse, assert workflow-id preserved
- `from-resource` with real JSON fixture in `test/resources/histories/`
- `from-resource` with missing resource throws `ex-info` with clear message
- `from-file` with a temp file
- `(datafy history)` returns map with `:workflow-id` and `:events`

Include fixture at `test/resources/histories/sample.json`.

### `test/temporal/test/replay_test.clj`
Integration tests using the `temporal.test.utils` fixture:

1. **Successful single replay:** Run a deterministic workflow, capture history with `history/fetch`, replay with `replayer/replay` â€” must not throw.
2. **Non-determinism detected:** Replay the same history against a modified workflow â€” must throw `ex-info` with `:workflow-id` in ex-data.
3. **`replay-all` all pass:** Replay 2-3 histories; assert `{:failures []}`.
4. **`replay-all` partial failure, collect-all:** `fail-fast? false`; assert `:failures` has one entry with correct `:workflow-id`.
5. **`replay-all` fail-fast:** `fail-fast? true`; assert stops early.
6. **`with-open` lifecycle:** Verify env is shut down after `with-open` block.

### Refactor `test/temporal/test/versioning.clj`
Replace raw Java interop at lines 49-56:
```clojure
;; Before:
(let [history (.fetchHistory client "test-1")]
  (.replayWorkflowExecution worker history))
;; After:
(let [h (history/fetch client "test-1")]
  (replayer/replay-history h))
```

## Conventions

```clojure
(ns temporal.test.replay-test
  (:require [clojure.test :refer [deftest testing is use-fixtures]]
            [temporal.test.utils :as t]
            [temporal.testing.history :as history]
            [temporal.testing.replayer :as replayer]))

(use-fixtures :once t/wrap-service)
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All unit tests pass without a live Temporal env
- [ ] All integration tests pass with the in-memory test env
- [ ] Refactored `versioning.clj` still passes all original assertions
- [ ] `lein test` (full suite) stays green
- [ ] Sample fixture `test/resources/histories/sample.json` checked in

title: Write replay testing documentation guide
content:
# Write replay testing documentation guide

Implements [[s-5c1w]]. Blocked by [[i-850r]], [[i-b5o1]], and [[i-9rsa]].

## Deliverables

### 1. Namespace and function docstrings

Both `temporal.testing.history` and `temporal.testing.replayer` must have:
- Namespace-level docstring explaining purpose and relationship between the two namespaces
- Per-function markdown docstrings: one-line summary, `Arguments:` table (`| Key | Description | Type | Default |`), fenced `clojure` example, `[[cross-refs]]`
- Model on `src/temporal/testing/env.clj`

### 2. `doc/replay_testing.md` guide

Structure:
```
# Replay Testing
## Why replay testing?
## The capture-then-replay workflow
## Loading and capturing histories
## Replaying a history
## Batch replay in CI
## Connection to versioning
## API reference
```

Link conventions:
- Upstream concepts: `[Temporal Testing Suite](https://docs.temporal.io/develop/java/testing-suite)`
- SDK API refs: `[temporal.testing.history/from-json](https://cljdoc.org/d/io.github.manetu/temporal-sdk/CURRENT/api/temporal.testing.history#from-json)`

### 3. `doc/cljdoc.edn` TOC entry

Add immediately after `["Testing" {:file "doc/testing.md"}]`:

```clojure
["Replay Testing" {:file "doc/replay_testing.md"}]
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `doc/replay_testing.md` exists and covers all sections
- [ ] All upstream links use `docs.temporal.io` URLs
- [ ] All SDK API links use `cljdoc.org/.../CURRENT/api/...` pattern
- [ ] `doc/cljdoc.edn` updated with TOC entry
- [ ] All public fns in both namespaces have proper docstrings
- [ ] Guide links back to `doc/workflows.md` versioning section
- [ ] `lein codox` renders without errors

title: Add replay-testing runnable sample
content:
# Add replay-testing runnable sample

Implements [[s-5c1w]]. Blocked by [[i-850r]], [[i-b5o1]], and [[i-9rsa]].

## Project structure

```
samples/replay-testing/
â”śâ”€â”€ project.clj
â”śâ”€â”€ README.md
â”śâ”€â”€ src/temporal/sample/replay_testing/core.clj
â””â”€â”€ test/
    â”śâ”€â”€ resources/histories/order.json   (pre-captured fixture)
    â””â”€â”€ temporal/sample/replay_testing/core_test.clj
```

## `src/.../core.clj`

A simple but realistic workflow demonstrating what replay testing protects:
- `defworkflow` with one or two `defactivity` calls (e.g. `validate-order`, `charge-payment`)
- Realistic enough to show the value; simple enough to follow

## `test/.../core_test.clj`

Full capture-then-replay cycle:

```clojure
(deftest replay-captures-determinism
  (testing "A valid history replays cleanly"
    (let [result @(c/get-result (c/start wf {:order-id "ord-1"}))]
      (is (= :ok (:status result)))
      (let [h (history/fetch client (workflow-id wf))]
        (spit "test/resources/histories/order.json" (history/->json h true))
        (is (nil? (replayer/replay-history (history/from-file "test/resources/histories/order.json")))))))
  (testing "Replay detects non-determinism"
    ;; replay a history against a modified workflow; assert throws
    ...))
```

Pre-capture `test/resources/histories/order.json` so tests are runnable without a live server.

## `README.md`

Model on `samples/hello-world/README.md`. Include:
- What this sample demonstrates
- `lein run` (run the workflow)
- `lein test` (run the replay test)
- Brief explanation of capture-then-replay with link to cljdoc guide and https://docs.temporal.io/develop/java/testing-suite

## `project.clj`

Model on `samples/hello-world/project.clj`. Depend on current SDK version. Include `eftest` in `:dev` profile.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `samples/replay-testing/` exists with full project structure
- [ ] `lein test` passes in the sample directory
- [ ] Tests run against in-memory `TestWorkflowEnvironment` (no real server)
- [ ] Pre-captured `order.json` fixture checked in
- [ ] `README.md` explains the sample and links to docs
- [ ] Compiles against current SDK version
